axis_responsibility.Admission: No constitutional or active-law mutation is proposed; checker/profile additions are medium-impact support pins and should stay within existing profile/kernel surfaces.
axis_responsibility.Agent: This return is design-lead support evidence only in closed AgentFact style; it does not classify success, failure, approval, or quality.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns the declared work statement and support/dashboard productization contract; no new Brick fact class or template change is proposed.
axis_responsibility.Evidence: Observed file content, Python-only projection probe output, and checker blockage are support evidence only.
axis_responsibility.Link: Movement vocabulary must remain `forward` / `reroute`; label changes must not choose Movement or route targets.
axis_responsibility.Support: Owns the dashboard server hygiene, deploy docs, bake function, and checker/profile mechanics as projection support only.

checker_or_verifier_plan: Temp-free shape probe now observed: `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; from brick_protocol.support.operator.dashboard_export import dashboard_export_packet; p=dashboard_export_packet(repo_root=Path('.')); print({'source_truth': p.get('source_truth'), 'buildings_is_list': isinstance(p.get('buildings'), list)})"`.

After implementation, add a Python-only bake probe that imports the new bake function, writes the fixed generated output path, reads it back with `json.load`, and asserts `source_truth is False` plus `isinstance(buildings, list)`.
Add a focused static Python probe for `server/index.mjs` requiring `NODE_ENV`, production branch, missing-secret refusal, `dev-secret` refusal, and `POST /ingest`-scoped refusal before body parse.

Add a hardcoded literal scanner for dashboard docs/app surfaces, excluding generated/package dependency files as declared, and mutate a copied file with a fake concrete project/URL to confirm RED behavior.

Run full gate in a normal writable-temp environment: `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --all`; current read-only sandbox produced exit 1 from tempdir unavailability, not from a dashboard assertion.
returned.design_summary: Design contract: keep the dashboard as a credential-free support projection that another company can deploy behind its own access wall. Add complete deploy documentation, production-only ingest refusal for missing or sentinel ingest secret, a synchronous Python bake function for the generated public packet, narrow movement/label polish, and checker-first pins with RED-style mutation probes. Do not add login, session storage, scheduler, queue, retry, source-truth behavior, Movement authority, or app-owned access control.
edge_cases: If a strict checker bans all URL literals under `support/dashboard`, existing Google Fonts URLs in `support/dashboard/index.html` need an explicit decision: exempt third-party font URLs or replace with local/system fonts. This is not the same as hardcoded deploy org/project/Cloud Run URL.
Legacy evidence may still contain `return` or `stop`; UI should display it as historical/lifecycle evidence rather than active Movement vocabulary.
Cloud Run scale-to-zero clears in-memory ingest state; baked `dashboard-data.json` must be the cold-start seed, and operators can re-bake from the repo ledger at any time.
Secret rotation is an operator procedure: update env secret, redeploy/revise service, update publishers, and let old revisions drain; the app should not store old or new secret bodies.
A production fail-closed branch should not block `/healthz`, static assets, `/events`, or baked `/dashboard-data.json`; only `POST /ingest` is refused.
The aggregate profile currently needs a writable temporary directory for some kernel checks; this sandbox cannot produce the full gate result.

invariants: Dashboard app stores no credentials and has only env-only `INGEST_SECRET` for `POST /ingest`.
Production `POST /ingest` fails closed when `INGEST_SECRET` is missing or sentinel `dev-secret`; static viewing and SSE stay available.
Seed baking reads persisted repo evidence and writes only generated `support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json`.
Baked dashboard packet must keep `source_truth` false and include a `buildings` list.
Deploy docs use placeholders or shell variables for organization, project, region, service, image, domain, and audience values; no company-specific URL or project literal is admitted.
Checker additions must include negative probes and must not rely only on text presence where mutation can silently stay accepted.
No Node-family command is required or wanted for proof in this attempt.

observed_evidence: No files were edited; `git status --short` returned no output in `/Users/smith/projects/BRICK-wt-dash`.

`support/dashboard/server/index.mjs` currently sets `INGEST_SECRET` to `process.env.INGEST_SECRET || 'dev-secret'` and checks only header equality before accepting `POST /ingest`; this does not yet express the production fail-closed branch.

`support/operator/dashboard_export.py` already computes `dashboard_export_packet()` with `source_truth: False` and top-level `buildings`; a Python-only temp-free probe observed `source_truth=False`, `buildings_is_list=True`, `building_count=9`, and required top keys present.
`support/operator/dashboard_export.py` has a CLI `--out` writer, but no named fixed bake function for `support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json` was observed.

`support/dashboard/src/data/labels.js` maps active/non-active movement-like words together: `forward`, `stop`, and `return`; `Link.jsx` displays movement as `전진 · 되돌림 · 멈춤` and counts `mv.return` / `mv.stop`.
`Building.jsx` and `graph.js` use display state `pass` and label `게이트 통과`; this is UI-internal display wording, but it is adjacent to protocol judgment vocabulary.

`support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml` is the current consolidated read-side projection profile; it admits existing dashboard surfaces and forbids new scheduler/queue/report-delivery-like support paths.
`support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py` already has dashboard export coverage inside the chat-session parked seam, but no focused dashboard productization kernel check was observed.

returned.output_excerpt: { "observed_evidence": [ "No files were edited; `git status --short` returned no output in `/Users/smith/projects/BRICK-wt-dash`.", "`support/dashboard/DEPLOY.md` is absent.", "`support/dashboard/server/index.mjs` currently sets `INGEST_SECRET` to `process.env.INGEST_SECRET || 'dev-secret'` and checks only header equality before accepting `POST /ingest`; this does not yet express the production fail-closed branch.", "`support/operator/dashboard_export.py` already computes `dashboard_export_packet()` with `source_truth: False` and top-level `buildings`; a Python-only temp-free probe observed...
proposed_changes: Create `support/dashboard/DEPLOY.md` fresh with parameterized Cloud Run + IAP commands, generic Docker behind an authenticating reverse proxy, and bare Node operation. Document seed baking, `INGEST_SECRET` rotation, scale-to-zero behavior, and source-truth boundary as repo ledger rather than dashboard memory.

In `support/dashboard/server/index.mjs`, split configured ingest secret from development fallback. In `NODE_ENV=production`, make `POST /ingest` refuse when the configured secret is missing or exactly `dev-secret`; leave static GET, `/healthz`, `/events`, and baked snapshot serving available.

Add a support-side function in `support/operator/dashboard_export.py`, for example `bake_dashboard_public_data(repo_root=REPO_ROOT, stale_days=STALE_DAYS_DEFAULT)`, that calls `dashboard_export_packet()` and writes `support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json` synchronously in one step.
Keep the existing generic `--out` CLI behavior, but add a dedicated bake CLI path only if it stays a thin wrapper over the new function and does not create a scheduler, poller, or runtime loop.

Polish movement labels narrowly: active Movement display should be `forward` and `reroute`; `return` should be marked historical if retained for legacy rows; `stop` should move out of Movement copy and be displayed as lifecycle/HOLD/paused state rather than a Movement literal.
Adjust `Link.jsx` movement card to count `mv.reroute` instead of `mv.return`, avoid presenting `stop` as a Movement, and source stopped/paused counts from display/lifecycle data if shown.
Optionally rename UI-internal gate display state `pass` / `게이트 통과` to non-verdict wording such as `넘김 관측` where it is only display projection; do not change BAL facts.

Add checker pins in the existing profile/kernel surfaces: static pin for the production fail-closed branch, static/no-literal scan for hardcoded deployment org/project/URL values under dashboard docs/app surfaces, and a Python bake output shape probe.

Add FIRE-style mutation probes in `kernel_checks.py` using a temp copy in normal checker environments: mutate the fail-closed branch and mutate forbidden deployment literals, expecting the focused checker to reject the changed copy.
relevant_current_structure: `support/dashboard/server/index.mjs` is a zero-dependency Node HTTP server for static app serving, `POST /ingest`, SSE `/events`, and baked `/dashboard-data.json` fallback.

`support/operator/dashboard_export.py` is the support read-side projection source for dashboard packets and already returns `source_truth: False`, proof limits, `not_proven`, summary, projects, buildings, agents, bricks, links, and detail.
`support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json` is treated as generated dashboard data and ignored by package path admission.

`support/dashboard/src/data/labels.js` is the central display-label table; `Link.jsx`, `Building.jsx`, and `graph.js` contain the visible movement/gate wording that currently leaks non-active or judgment-like vocabulary.

`support/checkers/check_profile.py`, `support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py`, and `support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml` are the likely checker/profile surfaces for this task; no new standalone `check_*.py` is needed.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No `agent/`, `brick/`, or `link/` changes are needed for the declared dashboard support task.
No `project/brick-protocol/buildings/**` or append-only project evidence mutation is part of the design.
No app login, account, session, credential store, database, scheduler, queue, retry, polling loop, Slack delivery, webhook delivery, or provider runtime should be introduced.
No npm, npx, node, vite, or build verification should be run under this working tree for this attempt.
The dashboard remains a support projection and does not become source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, Movement authority, route input, or target selector.
